title_en: WATCH: Scottish Lassie Completes Prep for Shuvee
description: Assistant news editor and BloodHorse Monday co-host Sean Collins goes behind the scenes with trainer Jorge Abreu July 23, the morning before Scottish Lassie runs in the $250,000 Shuvee Stakes (G2) July 24 at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Bing Crosby Stakes At a Glance: Cornucopian Formidable
description: Nine talented sprinters line up for the $400,000 Bing Crosby Stakes (G1) July 25 at Del Mar. The 6-furlong contest draws in two-time grade 3 winner Cornucopian and recent Triple Bend Stakes (G3) winner Stronghold as highlights.

title_en: Three Graded Stakes Highlight July 25 Woodbine Card
description: Woodbine shines the graded spotlight on female horses July 25, presenting three CA$175,000 stakes: the Ontario Colleen Stakes (G3T) for 3-year-old fillies at a mile on turf, followed by the Hendrie Stakes (G3) and the Trillium Stakes (G3) on Tapeta.

title_en: Prairie Meadows Announces $17M Community Grant
description: Prairie Meadows' board of directors announces that a $17 million community grant investment will provide funding to 473 nonprofit organizations that help serve the Iowa community. The grant is the largest in the organization's history.

title_en: Bidding Opens for Fasig-Tipton's July Digital Sale
description: Fasig-Tipton catalogs 146 racing and breeding prospects for its July Digital Sale, which opened for bidding July 23 and will close July 28. Maiden-breaking juveniles Velvet Beretta and impressive 9-length winner Cha Cha Baby are among the draft.

title_en: Benvenuto Cellini Faces Elders in Stellar King George
description: Aidan O'Brien has declared Benvenuto Cellini and Minnie Hauk among his team of four for one of the biggest races of the flat season in the King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes (G1) July 25 at Ascot Racecourse.

title_en: British Racing Bars Syndicate for Unpaid Prize Money
description: Deva Racing, whose director is Ryan Tongue, has allegedly failed to pay its syndicate members prize-money winnings and oversold shares in its horses.

title_en: NY Race Track Chaplaincy Brunch Aug. 12 in Saratoga
description: Donna Barton Brothers will return as master of ceremonies, and Joe Bianca will serve as the auctioneer when New York Race Track Chaplaincy hosts its 19th annual brunch Aug. 12 in Saratoga Springs, N.Y.

title_en: Built Seeks First Graded Win in Vanderbilt
description: Seeking his first graded victory, Eclipse Thoroughbred Partners' Built is tabbed as a clear 8-5 morning-line favorite in the $400,000 Alfred G. Vanderbilt Stakes (G2) July 25 at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Masquerade Ball's Trainer Confident Before King George
description: Japanese ace Masquerade Ball has yet to reach the ceiling of his abilities, according to his trainer, as he prepares for a rematch with Calandagan in a scintillating King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes (G1) July 25.

title_en: Expatriate Stallion Mutasaabeq Sires First Winner
description: John Guarnere's homebred Johnny G won in his second career start July 22 at Delaware Park.

title_en: Maryland Horseman Reightler Dies at 72
description: Maryland horseman Bill Reightler died July 21 from pancreatic cancer, according to a social media post from his daughter, Betsy Reightler.

title_en: Grand Job Looks to Finish the Job in Honorable Miss
description: After a pair of heartbreaking nose defeats, Grand Job aims for a return to the winner's circle in the $250,000 Honorable Miss Stakes (G2) July 26 at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: TOBA to Host Pedigree, Conformation Clinic
description: Veterinary, bloodstock, and sales professionals will share their expertise at the Thoroughbred Owners and Breeders Association's full-day Pedigree and Conformation Clinic on Oct. 19 at Fasig-Tipton in Lexington, Kentucky

title_en: Listenupshance Tops Crosby After True North Near Miss
description: Listenupshance, who threw a scare into the best eastern sprinters in the True North (G3) at Saratoga, could continue his rise through the ranks when he faces a stern test at 6 furlongs in the $400,000 Bing Crosby Stakes (G1) July 25 at Del Mar.

title_en: Reins of Hope Charity Raises More Than $50,000
description: The Reins of Hope charity auction, held July 10-21, generated more than $50,000 in proceeds to help those affected by the barn fire at Saratoga in June.

title_en: My Miss Mo, Jumping the Gun Clash in Monmouth Oaks
description: The 1-2 finishers from the George E. Mitchell Black-Eyed Susan Stakes (G2), My Miss Mo and Jumping the Gun, square off again in the $150,000 Monmouth Oaks (G3) July 25 at Monmouth Park.

description: The $500,000 Coaching Club American Oaks (G1) July 25 at Saratoga Race Course acts as a rubber match for Always a Runner and Counting Stars after they traded blows in the Kentucky Oaks (G1) and Acorn Stakes (G1).

title_en: Galloping Forward: Bourdieu's New Purpose
description: Bryce Bourdieu uses his passion for horse racing, dedication to rehab, and love of family and friends to recover and shift his career ambitions from riding horses to training them.

title_en: Stakes Recap: Haskell and Diana
description: Tom Amoss looks back at the Haskell Stakes at Monmouth Park & the Diana Stakes at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Tattersalls Unveils Somerville Yearling Catalog
description: The catalog for the sixth Tattersalls Somerville Yearling Sale, the auction that unearthed champion sprinter Bradsell, is now online and features siblings to 41 group and listed performers.

title_en: Multiple Leading Ohio Sire Dominus Dies at 18
description: Dominus, a graded stakes-winning son of Smart Strike, had to be euthanized after developing complications related to lymphoma.

title_en: Haskell-Winning Rider Vargas Named Jockey of the Week
description: After winning the Haskell Stakes (G1) on longshot Baby Vino, Jorge Vargas Jr. is Jockey of the Week for July 13-19.

description: Porter on Pedigrees examines the lineage of Baby Vino, winner of the Haskell Stakes and son of Spendthrift stallion Vino Rosso.

description: BloodHorse Daily highlights some of the top quotes of interest from industry-centered podcasts of July 15-21 and provides links to those shows.

description: Owner/breeder Simon Cox, 58, was chosen for the role ahead of former British Horseracing Board chair Peter Savill and Minette Batters.

title_en: Ascot Expecting 20,000 for 'Generational King George'
description: The July 25 main event is one of the highlights of the sport's summer, and features a clash of the generations as Calandagan, Masquerade Ball, and Benvenuto Cellini square off.

title_en: Scottish Lassie Targets More Saratoga Success in Shuvee
description: Scottish Lassie has won her past two Saratoga Race Course starts by a combined 24 lengths, and she aims to add to that margin July 24 in the $250,000 Shuvee Stakes (G2).

description: A rundown of this week’s horse racing coverage on TV, streaming, and radio as compiled by America's Best Racing.

description: Effusive and Salahudin are BloodHorse Maiden Watch Hot Prospects after victorious debuts last week.

description: Multiple graded stakes winner Journalism moves up after winning the July 18 San Diego Handicap (G2) at Del Mar.

description: Dollars & Sense with Frank Angst looks at how Woodbine and NYRA are partnering with technology companies to provide new handicapping and wagering tools for horseplayers.

title_en: Hawthorne Horsemen Hit with Triple Bankruptcy Whammy
description: The July 20 Hawthorne Race Course bankruptcy hearing provided a triple whammy for horsemen: Racing is over; there is no money to pay some $1 million in purses due to them; and they have to clear out from their backstretch home by Aug. 31.

title_en: Santa Anita Releases Autumn Meet Stakes Schedule
description: The 2026 Autumn Meet at Santa Anita Park will feature 26 stakes races that includes seven "Win and You're In" events for this year's Breeders' Cup. Santa Anita's 19-day Autumn Meet opens Sept. 25 and runs through Nov. 1.

description: On the July 20 episode of BloodHorse Monday: Lindsay Schultz on winning the Haskell Stakes (G1) with Baby Vino, Umberto Rispoli takes us through Journalism's San Diego Handicap (G2), and Atlantic Six's Jay Briscione on the honor of Book'em Danno Day.

title_en: Woodbine's E.P. Taylor Turf Course to Reopen Aug. 8

title_en: Study: Omeprazole Linked to Modest Increase in EIPH
description: The Hong Kong study involving 2,924 horses found a modest connection between daily treatments of the gastric treatment and preventative omeprazole and increased risk of exercise-induced pulmonary hemorrhaging, known commonly as bleeding.

title_en: Deceptive, Dam of Mizdirection, Dies at 27
description: Mizdirection won the Breeders' Cup Turf Sprint (G1) in 2012 and 2013, and retired with more than $1.7 million in earnings. She would sell later as a broodmare prospect for $2.7 million. She was the second stakes winner produced by Deceptive.

title_en: NTRA Calls for Restoring Full Deduction on Winnings
description: Citing handle declines this year, the NTRA sent a letter July 17 to U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent calling on the Treasury and the IRS to back legislation that would restore the gambling loss provision to the previous level.
